mnemonics.title: Bell-LaPadula Rules
mnemonics.content: SEND HELP: Simple Security Property = No Read Up (Higher to Lower). Star Property = No Write Down (Higher to Lower). This prevents data leakage from higher to lower security levels.

mnemonics.title: Biba Integrity Rules
mnemonics.content: CSI: Can't See Integrity = No Read Down. Can't Write Integrity = No Write Up. This keeps higher integrity data clean from contamination.

mnemonics.title: Security Goals
mnemonics.content: CIA TRIAD: Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability - the three fundamental goals of information security.

mnemonics.title: Authentication Factors
mnemonics.content: KHA: Knowledge (password), Have (smart card), Are (biometrics). These are the three main authentication factor types.

acronyms.usage: Attached to each object, specifies which subjects can access it and their permissions
Owner of resource decides who can access it and what operations are allowed
System enforces access rules based on security labels; users cannot override policies
Permissions assigned to roles; users assigned to roles based on job functions
System software that manages hardware resources and provides services to application programs
The set of all hardware, software, and firmware components responsible for enforcing security policies

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a spreadsheet where ROWS are users/domains, COLUMNS are files/resources, and CELL contents are permissions (read/write/execute). Easy to visualize who can access what.

Draw a pyramid with TOP = Top Secret, MIDDLE = Confidential, BOTTOM = Unclassified. Bell-LaPadula arrows go downward (no write down prevents leaking downward). Biba arrows go upward (no write up prevents contamination from below).

Think of a building with a restricted area (Kernel mode - requires special clearance) and public area (User mode - limited access). System calls are like secure doors between areas requiring verification.
Draw three concentric circles: OUTERMOST = User programs, MIDDLE = System programs/OS services, INNERMOST = Security Kernel/Reference Monitor. All access passes through the innermost circle.

analogies.analogy: PROTECTION is like internal house rules (who can use which room), while SECURITY is like the alarm system and locks that protect against outsiders.
ACL is like a guest list at a venue (checks each person at the door). Capability is like a ticket you carry that grants you specific access throughout the event.

Think of a military classification system. A General (high clearance) cannot discuss secret info with a Private (low clearance) - that's no write down. And a Private cannot access Top Secret files - that's no read up.
Like giving an employee only the keys and access cards they need for their specific job, not giving them master access to everything in the building.
Think of a bank vault with a single guard controlling all access. Every transaction must go through this guard, who checks credentials and authorizes each access attempt.
